workflow.name: brownfield-onboarding

critical.mandate: Document existing state before proposing changes
Stories should only cover gaps, not re-implement existing features
All diagrams must use Mermaid syntax — no ASCII art, no text descriptions of diagrams
All API documentation must use Swagger/OpenAPI format
Limit flow diagrams to 3-5 key flows to avoid output bloat

step.title: Deep Project Discovery
step.action: Invoke document-project workflow to scan codebase
Capture: tech stack, architecture, existing features, code quality, conventions
API Detection: scan for route definitions, controllers, OpenAPI/Swagger specs. Set {has_apis} flag (true/false)
Event Detection: scan for Kafka, RabbitMQ, SNS-SQS, Redis pub-sub, NATS, or other messaging patterns. Set {has_events} flag (true/false)
Dependency Detection: scan for outbound HTTP clients, SDKs, service URLs, database connections. Set {has_external_deps} flag (true/false)
Frontend Detection: scan for React, Angular, Vue, Flutter, SwiftUI, UI frameworks, CSS/styling. Set {has_frontend} flag (true/false)
Testing infrastructure: identify test framework, coverage config, test count, test patterns
CI/CD: identify GitHub Actions, Jenkins, Docker, Terraform, or other pipeline files

Generate brownfield assessment: read {installed_path}/../../templates/brownfield-assessment-template.md — capture component inventory, technical debt, migration constraints, coexistence strategy, and adoption path. Output to {planning_artifacts}/brownfield-assessment.md
step.template-output: Generate enhanced project documentation following the project documentation conventions. Include all standard sections plus: detected capability flags (has_apis, has_events, has_external_deps, has_frontend), testing infrastructure summary, and CI/CD pipeline summary.

step.title: API Documentation — Swagger/OpenAPI
step.action: Skip this step if {has_apis} is false. Log: "No API layer detected — skipping API documentation."
Read the API documentation template from {installed_path}/../../templates/api-documentation-template.md
Check if an OpenAPI/Swagger spec file already exists in the project (look for openapi.yaml, swagger.json, etc.)
If spec exists: validate it against actual route definitions, flag stale or missing entries
If no spec exists: generate an OpenAPI 3.x spec by scanning route definitions, types/DTOs, and middleware. Flag as "Generated from code scan — needs manual review"
Document all endpoints: method, path, handler, auth, parameters, request/response schemas, error formats
Include a Mermaid API flow diagram showing request lifecycle (client → middleware → handler → response)
List undocumented endpoints as gaps
step.template-output: Generate API documentation following the api-documentation-template. Include the OpenAPI spec (validated existing or generated), endpoint inventory table, authentication details, error format, Mermaid API flow diagram, and gap list. All API docs must be in Swagger/OpenAPI format.

step.title: UX Design Assessment
step.action: Skip this step if {has_frontend} is false. Log: "No frontend detected — skipping UX design assessment."
Read the UX design assessment template from {installed_path}/../../templates/ux-design-assessment-template.md
Document existing UI patterns: component library, design system, layout patterns, styling approach
Document navigation structure with a Mermaid sitemap diagram
Document interaction patterns: forms, modals, notifications, error handling, loading states
Assess accessibility: WCAG compliance level, ARIA usage, keyboard navigation, screen reader support
Identify UX gaps: missing responsive design, inconsistent patterns, accessibility violations
Propose UX improvements for gap features only — do not redesign working patterns
step.template-output: Generate UX design assessment following the ux-design-assessment-template. Include existing patterns, Mermaid navigation sitemap, interaction patterns, accessibility assessment, gap analysis, and proposed improvements for gaps only.

step.title: Event & Messaging Catalog
step.action: Skip this step if {has_events} is false. Log: "No event/messaging system detected — skipping event catalog."
Read the event catalog template from {installed_path}/../../templates/event-catalog-template.md
Document messaging infrastructure: system type, client library, configuration, serialization
Document produced events: event name, topic/queue, schema, trigger, publisher module
Document consumed events: event name, topic/queue, handler, error handling, consumer module
Document external events consumed from or produced for other services
Document delivery guarantees: at-least-once/at-most-once/exactly-once, retry policy, DLQ, idempotency
Include Mermaid event flow diagrams for 2-3 key event-driven flows
step.template-output: Generate event catalog following the event-catalog-template. Include messaging infrastructure, produced/consumed event tables, external events, delivery guarantees, and Mermaid event flow diagrams.

step.title: Dependency Map
step.action: Read the dependency map template from {installed_path}/../../templates/dependency-map-template.md
Document external service dependencies: APIs consumed, auth mechanism, health checks, error handling
Document infrastructure dependencies: databases, caches, search engines, storage, message queues
Document key library dependencies: ORM, auth lib, state management — check version currency and CVE risk
Create a Mermaid dependency graph showing the application and all its dependencies
Document contracts, SLAs, and fallback strategies where applicable
Identify dependency risks: outdated versions, missing health checks, single points of failure
step.template-output: Generate dependency map following the dependency-map-template. Include external service table, infrastructure table, key library table with CVE risk, Mermaid dependency graph, contracts/SLAs, and risk recommendations.
step.action: Spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/tasks/review-dependency-audit.xml. Read its entire contents. Follow the flow steps EXACTLY. Generate dependency audit report." This is required — the report feeds into the PRD gap analysis.
When subagent returns: verify dependency-audit-{date}.md exists in {planning_artifacts}/

step.title: NFR Assessment & Baselines
step.action: Read the NFR assessment template from {installed_path}/../../templates/nfr-assessment-template.md
Code quality: check for linting config, measure complexity hotspots, identify duplication patterns
Security posture: check dependency vulnerabilities, secrets handling, auth quality. Load security-basics skill JIT if needed (section: vulnerability-scanning)
Performance: assess bundle size (if frontend), query patterns, caching strategy, resource management
Accessibility: check ARIA usage, semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, a11y tooling (if frontend exists)
Test coverage: identify framework, count tests, check coverage %, find untested areas, assess test quality
CI/CD: document build pipeline, deploy strategy, environment management, infrastructure as code
Create NFR Baseline Summary Table with: category | current baseline | recommended target | gap severity
step.template-output: Generate NFR assessment following the nfr-assessment-template. Output to {test_artifacts}/nfr-assessment.md — this is the canonical location. Include code quality baselines, security posture, performance baselines, accessibility status, test coverage baselines, CI/CD assessment, and the NFR baseline summary table with measured values (not placeholders).

step.title: Performance Test Plan
step.action: REQUIRED for brownfield — create performance test plan
Load testing/knowledge/performance/k6-patterns.md for load testing patterns
Load testing/knowledge/performance/lighthouse-ci.md if {has_frontend} is true
Define performance budgets: P50/P95/P99 latency targets based on nfr-assessment.md baselines
Design load test scenarios: gradual load, spike test, soak test
If {has_frontend}: define Core Web Vitals targets (LCP under 2.5s, INP under 200ms, CLS under 0.1)
Identify backend profiling targets: slow queries, N+1 problems, connection pool sizing
Define CI performance gates with pass/fail thresholds
step.template-output: Generate performance test plan. Include: performance budgets derived from nfr-assessment.md baselines, load test scenarios (gradual, spike, soak), frontend performance targets (if has_frontend), backend profiling targets, and CI performance gate definitions.

step.title: Create PRD for Gaps
step.action: Read the PRD template from {installed_path}/../../templates/prd-template.md
Read upstream artifacts to inform gap analysis:
— nfr-assessment.md → NFR section gets real "Current Baseline" and "Target" columns
— api-documentation.md (if exists) → extract API gaps (undocumented endpoints, missing validation)
— event-catalog.md (if exists) → extract messaging gaps (missing DLQ, missing schemas)
— dependency-map.md → extract dependency risks (outdated versions, missing health checks)
— dependency-audit-{date}.md → extract critical/high findings as gap requirements (CVEs, outdated packages, license conflicts)
— ux-design.md (if exists) → extract UX gaps (accessibility violations, inconsistent patterns)
Invoke create-prd logic in brownfield mode: fill every section with gap-focused content only
Overview = existing project summary + what gaps this PRD addresses
Goals = gap closure goals only. Non-Goals = existing features that will NOT be re-implemented
User Stories = stories for gap features only, not existing functionality
Functional Requirements = gap requirements organized by priority
Non-Functional Requirements = NFR gaps with current baseline and target from {test_artifacts}/nfr-assessment.md

If {planning_artifacts}/prd.md already exists: WARN user — "A PRD already exists. Continuing will overwrite it with brownfield gap content. Choose: (a) overwrite, (b) save as prd-brownfield-gaps.md instead." If user chooses (b), adjust template-output path accordingly.
step.template-output: Generate a PRD following the standard template structure. Add "Mode: Brownfield — gaps only" to the header. All sections must follow the template format but contain only gap-focused content. NFR section must include "Current Baseline" and "Target" columns with real values from {test_artifacts}/nfr-assessment.md. Include a Priority Matrix section mapping each gap to priority/effort/impact.

step.title: Adversarial Review & PRD Refinement
step.action: Spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/tasks/review-adversarial.xml. Read its entire contents. Target: {planning_artifacts}/prd.md. Follow the task instructions EXACTLY." This is required — findings will be incorporated back into the PRD.
When subagent returns: verify adversarial-review-{date}.md exists in {planning_artifacts}/
Read {planning_artifacts}/adversarial-review-{date}.md — extract critical and high severity findings
For each critical/high finding: add as a new requirement or refine an existing requirement in the PRD
Add a "## Review Findings Incorporated" section to the PRD listing each finding, its severity, and how it was addressed
step.template-output: Update the PRD with adversarial review findings incorporated. Critical/high findings become new requirements or refine existing ones. Add "Review Findings Incorporated" section.

step.title: Architecture, Stories & Onboarding
step.action: Architecture and stories are created using the Phase 3 workflows which auto-detect brownfield mode from the PRD "Mode: Brownfield" header set in step 8.

Spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/3-solutioning/create-architecture/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Follow the workflow engine instructions EXACTLY." When subagent returns: verify {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md exists.

Spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/3-solutioning/create-epics-stories/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Follow the workflow engine instructions EXACTLY." When subagent returns: verify {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md and {planning_artifacts}/brownfield-onboarding.md exist.

workflow.next-step: Run /gaia-readiness-check in the Claude Code terminal to validate implementation readiness before starting development.
